title: ‘The Odyssey’ Stuns CinemaCon With Intense Trojan Horse Attack Footage; Christopher Nolan Says Movie Was an ‘Absolute Nightmare to Film — in All the Right Ways’
content:
ByRebecca Rubin

Senior Film and Media Reporter

The Patron Saint of Movie Theaters has returned toCinemaCon, the exhibition industry’s annual trade show.

Christopher Nolanmade his way to Las Vegas to talk up “The Odyssey,” his historical drama based on Homer’s Greek epic. Not that he needs to convince exhibitors to schedule any of his films. Movie theater operators adore Nolan because he’s the rare director who can get people to the big screen based on his name alone.

Naturally, he was greeted with a standing ovation as he walked on stage at the Colosseum. An introvert, the filmmaker tried to deflect the attention, joking that he’s just happy that he’s not following Steven Spielberg during Universal’s presentation to exhibitors. (Spielberg’s new film “Disclosure Day” was teased later in the afternoon.)

He got right to the point, addressing his decision to adapt the original epic for today’s audiences. Matt Damon stars as Odysseus, the king of Ithaca, and the story chronicles his long and perilous return home after the Trojan War.

“Why ‘The Odyssey?’ ‘The Odyssey’ is a story that has fascinated generation after generation for 3,000 years,” Nolan mused. “It’s not a story. It’sthestory.”

Nolan treated exhibitors to an extended look at “The Odyssey,” which opened with Damon’s Odysseus, shirtless on the beach with a burly beard. He’s been gone a long time and admits to Calypso (Charlize Theron) that he “can’t remember anything before Troy.”“Did I have a wife? Children? Maybe a son?” he asks. “If I had a son, how old would he be now?”

Most of the footage revolved around “the story of the horse” — the famous Trojan horse attack, which the Greeks used as cover to enter the city of Troy at the end of the war. As the massive wooden figure washes up on the beach, thousands of men have to pull the Trojan horse out of the water and onto the sand before they can wheel it into the city. In the tense and thrilling sequence, the Greeks are forced to stay quiet as the Trojans plunge swords into the statue to see if anything was hidden, with one blade even slicing into the face of a hidden soldier.

Later, there are shots of Odysseus urging his soldiers to keep rowing through a storm, and then slinging arrows as they launch their full-scale attack in the dark of night. The footage ended with a quick shot of Odysseus and his soldiers as they face the man-eating cyclops Polyphemus; the massive brute picks up a man in his hand before the screen cuts to black. He’s one of many mythical beings — Cyclops, sirens and Circe among them — who Odysseus will encounter as he ventures to reunite with his wife Penelope (Anne Hathaway) and son Telemachus (Tom Holland).

Along with Damon, Hathaway, Holland and Theron, the stacked ensemble includes Zendaya, Lupita Nyong’o, Robert Pattinson, Jon Bernthal, Benny Safdie, John Leguizamo and Himesh Patel. Given the robust call sheet, Nolan joked there were too many stars to feasibly bring with him to CinemaCon.

“How do you go about bringing this to a modern audience? Obviously, we start with the cast,” Nolan said. “It’ll be quicker for me to tell you whoisn’tin the movie. I would have brought them all here, but the massive weight of extraordinary talent would have collapsed the stage.”

Nolan described a treacherous filming process, which brought them all over the world as they shot across Morocco, Greece, Italy, Iceland and Scotland.

“This has been an absolute nightmare to film — but in all the right ways,” he said. “We had an amazing time.” He singled out his leading man, referring to Damon as his “partner on this journey” and calling his work “incredible.”

“He was there on the boats, up the mountains, in the caves, in the beating sunshine, sideways rain, wind,” Nolan said. “You’ll be pleased to know how difficult it was. It was meant to be; that’s the nature of this story.”

“The Odyssey” will be the first movie shot entirely with Imax cameras, a feat that Nolan called his “longest-held ambition.” His prior blockbuster, “Oppenheimer,” was particularly huge in Imax, with the premium format contributing a massive 20% of overall box office grosses.Some movie lovers even crossed state linesto see “Oppenheimer” in 70mm Imax, selling out those particularly large auditoriums for weeks. Nolan’s other major films include the “Dark Knight” trilogy, “Inception” and “Interstellar.”

“As a boy, all I wanted to do was tell large-scale [stories] using that technology, putting the audience into the world,” Nolan said in reference to Imax. “And I spent many, many years trying to bring that to fruition, starting with ‘The Dark Knight,’ back when I was in my 30s. We shot the action sequences [in Imax], but we were never able to shoot the entire film. My crew did an incredible job figuring out how to do this for the first time.”
title_zh: 《奧德賽(The Odyssey)》在CinemaCon上以激烈的特洛伊木馬攻擊畫面震撼全場；克里斯多福·諾蘭(Christopher Nolan)表示這部電影拍攝過程是「絕對的噩夢——但在所有正確的方式上」。
content_zh:
由瑞貝卡·魯賓(Rebecca Rubin)報導

高級電影與媒體記者

電影劇院的守護聖人回到了CinemaCon，這個展覽業的年度貿易展。

克里斯多福·諾蘭(Christopher Nolan)來到拉斯維加斯，宣傳他的歷史劇《奧德賽》(The Odyssey)，該片改編自荷馬的希臘史詩。其實，他不需要說服放映商排片，因為電影放映商們非常喜愛諾蘭，因為他是少數能夠僅憑名字就吸引觀眾走進大銀幕的導演。

當他走上科洛西姆舞台時，自然獲得了熱烈的掌聲。這位內向的導演試圖轉移注意力，開玩笑說他很高興自己不是在環球影業的放映商介紹中接著史蒂芬·史匹柏(Steven Spielberg)的演講。（史匹柏的新片《揭露日》(Disclosure Day)稍後在下午被提及。）

他直入主題，談到他為當今觀眾改編原始史詩的決定。馬特·達蒙(Matt Damon)飾演伊薩卡的國王奧德修斯，故事講述了他在特洛伊戰爭後漫長而危險的歸家之旅。

“為什麼選擇《奧德賽》？《奧德賽》是一個吸引了三千年世世代代的故事，”諾蘭沉思道。“這不是一個故事，而是故事。”

諾蘭為放映商們呈現了《奧德賽》的延長片段，片段開場是達蒙飾演的奧德修斯，赤裸上身，滿臉鬍鬚地坐在沙灘上。他已經離開很久，並向卡莉普索(查理茲·塞隆(Charlize Theron))承認他“無法記起特洛伊之前的任何事情。”他問道：“我有妻子嗎？孩子？也許有個兒子？”“如果我有個兒子，他現在幾歲？”

大部分片段圍繞著“馬的故事”——著名的特洛伊木馬攻擊，希臘人利用這個掩護進入特洛伊城。在這個巨大的木製雕像沖上沙灘時，成千上萬的男人必須將特洛伊木馬從水中拉到沙灘上，然後才能將其推入城市。在這個緊張而刺激的場景中，希臘人被迫保持安靜，因為特洛伊人用劍刺入雕像，看看裡面是否隱藏著什麼，甚至有一把刀划破了隱藏士兵的臉。

隨後，畫面顯示奧德修斯催促他的士兵在暴風雨中繼續划船，然後在黑夜中發射箭矢，展開全面攻擊。片段最後快速切換到奧德修斯和他的士兵面對吃人獨眼巨人波里斐摩斯(Polyphemus)的畫面；這位巨人用手抓起一名男子，然後畫面切黑。他是奧德修斯在尋找妻子佩內洛普(安·海瑟薇(Anne Hathaway))和兒子特勒馬科斯(Tom Holland)的旅程中將遇到的許多神話生物之一——獨眼巨人、海妖和基爾克(Kirke)等。

除了達蒙、海瑟薇、荷蘭和塞隆外，這部陣容強大的影片還包括辛迪亞(Zendaya)、露皮塔·尼永奧(Lupita Nyong’o)、羅伯特·帕丁森(Robert Pattinson)、喬恩·伯恩薩爾(Jon Bernthal)、班尼·薩夫迪(Benny Safdie)、約翰·雷吉薇茲(John Leguizamo)和希梅什·帕特爾(Himesh Patel)。考慮到這份強大的演員陣容，諾蘭開玩笑說，帶來CinemaCon的明星實在太多了。

「你們如何將這部作品帶給現代觀眾呢？顯然，我們首先要從演員陣容開始。」克里斯多福·諾蘭(Christopher Nolan)說。「告訴你這部電影裡誰不在，比告訴你誰在裡面要快得多。我本想把他們都帶來，但超凡的才華重量太大，會讓舞台崩塌。」

諾蘭描述了艱難的拍攝過程，他們在摩洛哥、希臘、義大利、冰島和蘇格蘭等地拍攝，遍及全球。

「這部電影的拍攝絕對是一場噩夢——但都是在正確的方式下。」他說。「我們度過了一段美好的時光。」他特別提到他的男主角，稱馬特·達蒙(Matt Damon)為他「這段旅程的夥伴」，並稱讚他的表現「不可思議」。

「他在船上、山上、洞穴裡、烈日下、側雨中、風中都在。」諾蘭說。「你會高興地知道這有多困難。這是必然的；這就是這個故事的本質。」

《奧德賽(The Odyssey)》將是第一部完全使用IMAX攝影機拍攝的電影，這一成就被諾蘭稱為他「長久以來的抱負」。他之前的大片《奧本海默(Oppenheimer)》在IMAX格式中表現特別出色，這一高端格式貢獻了整體票房的20%。一些電影愛好者甚至跨州前往觀看《奧本海默》的70mm IMAX版本，這些特別大的放映廳連續幾周都售罄。諾蘭的其他重要作品包括《黑暗騎士三部曲(The Dark Knight trilogy)》、《全面啟動(Inception)》和《星際穿越(Interstellar)》。

「作為一個男孩，我唯一想做的就是利用那種技術講述大型故事，將觀眾帶入這個世界。」諾蘭提到IMAX時說。「我花了很多年試圖實現這一點，從《黑暗騎士(The Dark Knight)》開始，那時我還在30多歲。我們拍攝了動作場景[在IMAX中]，但從未能拍攝整部電影。我的團隊在首次實現這一點上做得非常出色。」
summary_zh:
由瑞貝卡·魯賓(Rebecca Rubin)報導

高級電影與媒體記者

電影劇院的守護聖人回到了CinemaCon，這個展覽業的年度貿易展。

克里斯多福·諾蘭(Christopher Nolan)來到拉斯維加斯，宣傳他的歷史劇《奧德賽》(The Odyssey)，該片改編自荷馬的希臘史詩。
